description: This figure consists of two subplots investigating stacking fault energy (SFE) of three austenitic steel alloys: base alloy, 2.5 wt.% Si alloyed alloy, and 2.5 wt.% Al alloyed alloy. Subplot (a) plots partial dislocation separation (unit: nm) as a function of perfect dislocation angle (unit: deg., 0 to 90 deg.). Triangles represent measured data for 2.5 Si alloy, circles for base alloy, squares for 2.5 Al alloy. Solid lines are calculated trends based on average SFE, dashed lines are upper/lower bounds from SFE standard deviation, with measured SFE values 19.9±2.0 mJ/m² for 2.5 Si, 26.3±3.0 mJ/m² for base, 38.3±6.1 mJ/m² for 2.5 Al. Subplot (b) shows SFE dependence on relative alloying content, indicating 2.5 wt.% Al addition increases SFE by 12.0 mJ/m² from base, while 2.5 wt.% Si addition reduces SFE by 6.4 mJ/m². This figure confirms Al alloying increases while Si alloying reduces SFE of austenitic steels, consistent with observed partial dislocation spacing trends.
